rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CAIN ET AL VS. HILL BROTHERS CHEMICAL COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Franklin Truck Parts, Inc. S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgement
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 25, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Franklin Truck Parts Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Franklin Truck Parts Inc. shifted burden to Plaintiffs by providing evidence that Matthew Cain identified the Franklin Truck Parts store in Fontana, California. Matthew Cain is the only witness to Decedent's exposure and insisted that his father purchased truck parts at no other location except the store located in Fontana, California. (See Decl. McGahey, Ex. C at p. 5; Decl. McGahey, Ex. I, Depo. Matthew Cain at pp. 84:10-18, 93:8-18, 105:17-22, 122:19-123:2, 139:11-140:24.) Mathew Cain testified that the purchases occurred in the 1980s. (Decl. McGahey, Ex. I, Depo. Matthew Cain at pp. 84:10-18, 93:8-18, 105:17-22, 132:12-14.) Franklin Truck Parts provided affirmative evidence that it did not even purchase the land where the store was build on until 1991 and did not open the store until approximately 1992. (Decl. McGahey, Ex. J, Ex. K, Ex. M.) Thus, Franklin Truck Parts Inc. shifted the burden. (See CCP § 437c(p)(2); see McGonnel v.Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc. (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 1098, 1103.) Plaintiffs provided sufficient evidence to demonstrate a triable issue of material fact. (See CCP § 437(p)(2); see Saelzer v. Advanced Group (2001) 25 Cal.4th 763, 780-781.) The declaration of Matthew Cain and copies of Riverside phone books from 1982 and 1986 demonstrate that Decedent may have purchased products from Franklin Truck Parts at a different location other than non-existent location in Fontana, California. (Decl. Renken, Ex. B, Decl. Matthew Cain 6; Decl. Renken 4, Ex. C.) The Court does not disregard Matthew Cain's declaration under Whitmire v. Ingersoll-Rand Co. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1078. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
